overview.short: The Lakshmeshwara Jain temples is a group of Jain temples in the town of Lakshmeshwara in the Gadag district of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Lakshmeshwara
topPlaces.description: Lakshmeshwara (historically known as Puligere) is a town, and newly created Taluk place along with Gajendragad in Gadag district, in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is about 40 km from Gadag and 55 km from Hubli.

topPlaces.name: Someshwara Temple, Lakshmeshwara
topPlaces.description: Someshwara Temple, Lakshmeshwara is a temple in Lakshmeshwara. It was restored between 2012 and 2015, with the restoration supervised by the Archaeological Survey of India and funded by the Infosys foundation.

topPlaces.name: Haralapur
topPlaces.description: Haralapur is a village in Dharwad district of Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Kalasa, Kundgol
topPlaces.description: Gudgeri is a village in the Kundgol taluk of Dharwad district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Kalas, Karnataka
topPlaces.description: Kalas is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kundgol taluk of Dharwad district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Yalvigi
topPlaces.description: Yalvigi is a city in Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Yalavagi
topPlaces.description: Yalavagi is a village in savanur taluk Haveri district, India. Yalavagi village is Railway station Stoppage to Connecting the Laxmeshwar Town [13;Km] And Gadag[53;Km].

topPlaces.name: Shigli
topPlaces.description: Shigli is a city located in the Shirhatti taluk of Gadag district in Karnataka. It is situated 52 kilometres (32 mi) to the south of district headquarters Gadag, 26 km (16 mi) from Shirhatti, and 382 km (237 mi) from state capital Bangalore.

items.note: Lakshmeshwara (historically known as Puligere) is a town, and newly created Taluk place along with Gajendragad in Gadag district, in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is about 40 km from Gadag and 55 km from Hubli.

Someshwara Temple, Lakshmeshwara is a temple in Lakshmeshwara. It was restored between 2012 and 2015, with the restoration supervised by the Archaeological Survey of India and funded by the Infosys foundation.
Haralapur is a village in Dharwad district of Karnataka, India.
Gudgeri is a village in the Kundgol taluk of Dharwad district in the Indian state of Karnataka.
Kalas is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kundgol taluk of Dharwad district in Karnataka.
Yalvigi is a city in Karnataka, India.
Yalavagi is a village in savanur taluk Haveri district, India. Yalavagi village is Railway station Stoppage to Connecting the Laxmeshwar Town [13;Km] And Gadag[53;Km].

Shigli is a city located in the Shirhatti taluk of Gadag district in Karnataka. It is situated 52 kilometres (32 mi) to the south of district headquarters Gadag, 26 km (16 mi) from Shirhatti, and 382 km (237 mi) from state capital Bangalore.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lakshmeshwara Jain temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lakshmeshwara Jain temples is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lakshmeshwara Jain temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (48 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (43 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lakshmeshwara Jain temples?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lakshmeshwara Jain temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Lakshmeshwara Jain temples?
faq.a: Stays in Lakshmeshwara Jain temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Lakshmeshwara Jain temples?
faq.a: The top attractions are Lakshmeshwara, Someshwara Temple, Lakshmeshwara, Haralapur, Kalasa, Kundgol, Kalas, Karnataka.
